data.title: Trump Media makes Truth+ on-demand content available on Roku TV
data.description:
Trump Media and Technology (NASDAQ:DJT) said Thursday that on-demand TV streaming content for Truth+ is now accessible on connected TV apps for Roku (NASDAQ:ROKU) TV sets.

The move by Trump Media complements its live TV offering, which it made available on the Roku app in March of this year.

The company also said all Truth+ programming will now be available on Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) connected TVs manufactured since 2022 as well as LG connected TVs, on top of iOS devices, Android devices, Apple TVs, Android TVs, Amazon Fire TVs, and on the Web.

data.title: NV Gold announces C$500,000 financing and debt settlement
data.description:
• NV Gold (TSXV:NVX:CA) announced on Thursday a non-brokered private placement for gross proceeds of up to C$500,000 and the deleveraging of its balance sheet with the conversion of loans in the aggregate amount of $400,000 into equity.
  • The company announces a non-brokered private placement offering of up to 5,000,000 units at a price of C$0.10 per unit for gross proceeds of up to C$500,000.
  • Each unit consists of one common share of the company and one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to acquire one additional common share at a price of C$0.20 for a period of 24 months from the date of issuance, said the company. 
  • The company will use the proceeds of the offering for exploration activities at its Slumber Gold Project and for general working capital purposes.
  • The company said it has completed its previously announced shares for debt transaction and issued an aggregate of 5,161,578 common shares of the company at C$0.1125 per share in settlement of payment owed pursuant to loan agreements with John Watson, President, Chairman, CEO and a director of the company, totaling $419,050 (C$580,677.58). 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ralph Lauren beats forecasts, raises dividend as resilience pays off
data.description:
Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) delivered another strong quarter, outperforming Wall Street’s expectations in its fiscal fourth quarter, buoyed by a resilient supply chain and solid balance sheet, leading the company to issue upbeat guidance and raise its quarterly dividend by 10%.

“Our strong performance in the third and final year of our Next Great Chapter: Accelerate plan underscores the growing desirability of our brand and our team's powerful execution as we navigated a dynamic global operating environment," said Ralph Lauren CEO Patrice Louvet.

An 8.3% increase in total revenue to $1.7B was fueled by direct-to-consumer sales and 5.9M new DTC customers, as well as by sales growth in its three major regions (North America, Europe, Asia). Notably, DTC sales in Europe were up 25%, up 15% in Asia, and increased 8% in North America. Coupled with solid growth in its brick-and-mortar channel, total comparable sales increased 13% in FQ4, accelerating from a 10% increase in the same quarter last year.

As a result of increased sales, favorable product and channel mix, and lower cotton costs that more than offset higher costs for non-cotton products, Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) earned a profit of $2.27 per share, an increase of 32% from a year ago and $0.23 better than expected. The company’s adjusted profit margin expanded by 200 basis points to 68.6%.

On the balance sheet, Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) ended the quarter with $300M more in cash and short-term investments for a total of $2.1B.

Besides a higher dividend, Ralph Lauren (RL) will also return cash to shareholders through its share buyback program, increased by $1.5B.

For the fiscal first quarter, based on the current geopolitical and macroeconomic environment, revenue is expected to increase by high-single-digits with a 150-200 basis point increase to its operating margin.

For FY26, revenue is expected to increase by low-single digits over last year, with a “modest” expansion in its operating margin and unchanged gross margin as lower input costs and AUR growth offsetting the negative impact from increased tariffs.

“As we enter fiscal 2026, we remain on offense — with a focus on driving our multiple engines of growth across lifestyle categories, geographies, and channels. At the same time, we will stay agile and prudent — leaning into our diversified supply chain, operating discipline, and strong balance sheet as we manage through ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty,” Louvet added.

data.title: Philip Morris is still blazing hot with analysts and quant scores despite being at an all-time high
data.description:
Needham initiated coverage of Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) on Thursday with a Buy rating.

Analyst Gerald Pascarelli noted that Philip Morris (NYSE:PM) has already made great strides in transforming towards a smoke-free business, with about ~40% of revenue and profits generated from new product lines. Pascarelli said PM has solidified itself as the market leader in both heated tobacco and nicotine pouches, which he noted are two of the healthiest categories across the consumer landscape that also carry a higher margin profile relative to combustibles. 

"Through increased consumer adoption, PM continues to deliver consistent DD growth in legacy smoke-free markets, which along with better performance in combustibles, has translated to four consecutive years of positive consolidated volume growth," wrote Pascarelli. "Market leadership, innovation, and geographic whitespace should provide a long runway for PM to drive strong, mix accretive revenue growth, which should translate to a sustainable HSDLDD earnings growth profile," he added.

Needham assigned a price target of $195 to Philip Morris.

Philip Morris (PM) has a consensus Buy rating with Wall Street and Seeking analysts, as well as having the highest Seeking Alpha Quant score of any consumer staples stock.

Shares of Philip Morris (PM) traded flat in the premarket session on Thursday at $174.58.

data.title: Smaller IT firms see opportunity amid Trump's DOGE streamlining: WSJ
data.description:
Smaller government software contractors like Appian (NASDAQ:APPN) and Pegasystems (NASDAQ:PEGA) are eyeing new business opportunities as the Trump administration pushes for a leaner, more tech-driven federal bureaucracy, The Wall Street Journal reported Thursday.

The smaller firms must also be cautious. They could benefit from the shift or find themselves facing the same budgetary axe that’s trimming others.

Leaders from both companies have publicly expressed support for the aims of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), a reform initiative championed by Elon Musk that emphasizes technological modernization across federal agencies.

Appian (NASDAQ:APPN) Chief Executive Matt Calkins said to the Journal that his company is actively collaborating with DOGE across multiple agencies.

Alan Trefler, chief executive of Pegasystems (NASDAQ:PEGA), echoed that sentiment, saying DOGE’s emphasis on digitization and cutting outdated systems aligns closely with Pegasystems’ (PEGA) core mission: automating workflows and eliminating inefficiencies.

DOGE’s efforts have led to job reductions, contract cancellations and a push for centralized control over government IT infrastructure. The initiative heavily leverages automation and AI, making it a major player in reshaping the way federal agencies operate.

This overhaul has put pressure on large federal contractors to demonstrate their value. For smaller firms like Appian (APPN), however, it’s a rare chance to challenge the dominance of entrenched, deep-pocketed vendors.

Calkins said his company in the past felt disadvantaged as a smaller company without legacy relationships.

Appian’s (APPN) software helps automate repetitive tasks, often referred to as process mining, and is currently used by branches of the U.S. military, including the Air Force, Army and Navy, for procurement-related tasks.

Despite DOGE’s vision seemingly aligning with Appian’s (APPN) offering, investor concerns linger. TD Cowen analyst Derrick Wood noted that DOGE’s scrutiny of federal IT contracts could potentially deem some services non-essential.

Calkins acknowledged that uncertainty during a recent earnings call, while pointing to the company's significant federal footprint. One of Appian’s (APPN) Education Department contracts has already appeared on a DOGE cancellation list, based on data compiled by federal contracting site HigherGov cited by the Journal.

Pegasystems (PEGA), which also provides digital process automation tools to federal agencies, may see impacts from changes in government tech budgets, analysts at Citi have suggested.

DOGE has reported canceling about $30 million in contracts linked to digital modernization. Still, overall federal spending has risen under President Trump, according to the Journal.

data.title: GCL secures $2.9 million senior unsecured convertible note financing
data.description:
GCL Global (NASDAQ:GCL) Thursday said that it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with ATW Partners for the issuance of senior unsecured convertible notes, through a facility of up to $45.5 million.

Pursuant to the SPA, the company will issue an initial note in the aggregate original principal amount of $2,900,000, at a purchase price of $2,610,000.

GCL has the right to require the investor to purchase additional notes in the aggregate original principal amount of up to $42,600,000, at a purchase price of $38,340,000 under the facility. 

The Notes will have a three-year term and bear interest at 6% per annum, payable monthly, at GCL’s option, in cash or, provided that certain conditions are met, in GCL’s ordinary shares.

The proceeds shall be used for general corporate and working capital purposes.

data.title: Initial jobless claims unexpectedly slip by 2K in past week
data.description:
Initial jobless claims for the week ended May 17: -2K to 227K vs. 230K consensus and 229K prior (unrevised), according to data released by the Department of Labor on Thursday.

Continuing claims: 1.903M vs. 1.890M consensus and 1.867M prior (revised from 1.881M).

Developing...check back for updates.

data.title: Chicago Fed National Activity Index reverses to negative territory in April
data.description: The Chicago Fed National Activity Index ("CFNAI") fell to -0.25 in April, reversing from +0.03 in the prior month, which was revised from -0.03, the Chicago Fed said on Thursday.

data.title: Ingredion declares $0.80 dividend
data.description:
• Ingredion (NYSE:INGR) declares $0.80/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.31%
  • Payable July 22; for shareholders of record July 1; ex-div July 1.
  • See INGR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: TotalEnergies launches its largest European solar project
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said Thursday it has started up its largest solar project in Europe, a cluster of five solar projects near Sevilla in Spain with a total installed capacity of 263 MW.

The solar field will produce 515 GWh/year of renewable electricity, equivalent to the consumption of more than 150K Spanish households, and will avoid 245k tons/year of carbon emissions, TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said, adding that most of the electricity produced will be sold through long-term power purchase agreements and the rest will be sold on the wholesale market.

The installation of 400K bifacial solar panels with trackers during construction was declared of strategic interest by the regional Andalusian government and has provided a significant economic boost to the local economy, the company said.

TotalEnergies (TTE) said it has 28 GW of gross installed renewable electricity generation capacity worldwide, with a goal of reaching 35 GW by year-end 2025, part of a strategy to expand investment in renewables alongside oil and gas.
